quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: Organizing the Hierarchy of Quadrilaterals
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 6.10 Review of Quadrilateral Family Tree

questions.question: Which statement about the quadrilateral hierarchy is correct?
options: All squares are rectangles, but not all rectangles are squares
All rectangles are squares, but not all squares are rectangles
All rhombuses are squares, but not all squares are rhombuses
All parallelograms are rectangles, but not all rectangles are parallelograms
questions.explanation: A square has all the properties of a rectangle (four right angles) plus the additional property of four congruent sides. Therefore, every square is a rectangle, but a rectangle without four congruent sides is not a square.

questions.question: Which statement about the quadrilateral hierarchy is correct?
options: All squares are rhombuses, but not all rhombuses are squares
All rhombuses are squares, but not all squares are rhombuses
All kites are rhombuses, but not all rhombuses are kites
All trapezoids are parallelograms, but not all parallelograms are trapezoids
questions.explanation: A square has all the properties of a rhombus (four congruent sides) plus the additional property of four right angles. Every square is a rhombus, but a rhombus without right angles is not a square.

questions.question: Which quadrilateral is a subset of both rectangles and rhombuses?
questions.explanation: A square is the intersection of rectangles and rhombuses. It has the four right angles of a rectangle and the four congruent sides of a rhombus.

questions.question: Under the inclusive definition (a trapezoid has AT LEAST one pair of parallel sides), which statement correctly describes the relationship between parallelograms and trapezoids?
options: All parallelograms are trapezoids, but not all trapezoids are parallelograms
All trapezoids are parallelograms, but not all parallelograms are trapezoids
Trapezoids and parallelograms are completely separate categories with no overlap
Trapezoids and parallelograms are identical categories with the same requirements
questions.explanation: Under the inclusive definition, a trapezoid requires at least one pair of parallel sides. Since a parallelogram has two pairs of parallel sides, it automatically satisfies the trapezoid requirement. However, a trapezoid with only one pair of parallel sides is not a parallelogram.

questions.question: A rhombus is a parallelogram with which additional property?
options: All four angles are right angles
Exactly one pair of opposite sides is parallel
questions.explanation: A rhombus inherits all parallelogram properties and adds the condition that all four sides are congruent. This gives it perpendicular diagonals and angle-bisecting diagonals.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides, so it is not a parallelogram. Rectangles, rhombuses, and squares all have two pairs of parallel sides and are subsets of parallelograms.

questions.question: Which list correctly orders quadrilaterals from most general to most specific?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy flows from general to specific: quadrilateral (any four-sided figure) → parallelogram (opposite sides parallel) → rectangle (parallelogram with right angles) → square (rectangle with congruent sides).

questions.question: A quadrilateral is both a rhombus and a rectangle. What must it be?
questions.explanation: The intersection of the set of rhombuses (four congruent sides) and the set of rectangles (four right angles) is exactly the set of squares.

questions.question: Which property is shared by all parallelograms, rectangles, rhombuses, and squares?
questions.explanation: All parallelograms have diagonals that bisect each other. Congruent diagonals are specific to rectangles, perpendicular diagonals to rhombuses, and four congruent sides to rhombuses.

questions.question: A kite has two pairs of adjacent congruent sides. Which quadrilateral is always a kite?
questions.explanation: A rhombus has all four sides congruent, which means it also has two distinct pairs of adjacent congruent sides. Therefore, every rhombus is a kite, but not every kite is a rhombus.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the quadrilateral family tree and can navigate the hierarchy with confidence.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main relationships but review the intersection of rhombuses and rectangles (squares) and whether trapezoids are parallelograms.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: quadrilateral → parallelogram → rectangle/rhombus → square. A square is always a rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, kite, and quadrilateral.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The hierarchy: all squares are rectangles and rhombuses; all rectangles and rhombuses are parallelograms; all parallelograms are quadrilaterals. Kites and trapezoids are separate branches.
